title: Peppery Kenyan Buckwheat Flatbread
description: A peppery and crumbly flatbread featuring buckwheat flour, perfect for a date night, with a flexitarian-friendly mother sauce base.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the buckwheat flour, all purpose flour, black pepper, and salt.
In a separate bowl, mix the yeast and water, then let it sit for 5 minutes.
Add the yeast mixture to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rest for 30 minutes in a warm place.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Brush the tops with olive oil and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Serve the flatbread with the mother sauce and enjoy.
